context: User report: 'find_symbol is used incorrectly. Agent should be able to navigate via semantic/hybrid_search, then use find_symbol, but Opus 4.7 does not do this.' Additionally: agent should understand find_symbol can show documentation TOC.

audit_findings.AUDIT-1: Decision Tree has 'Already have search results → NAVIGATE → find_symbol' branch but without explicit triggers WHEN this applies.
audit_findings.AUDIT-2: 'Chunk is the source of truth. Do not re-read files' reads as 'don't do anything with result'. No distinction between Read-to-verify (bad) and find_symbol-to-navigate (good).
audit_findings.AUDIT-3: Navigation chain example (line 299-305) is at the bottom — Wave 2 confirms Opus 4.7 skips content below line ~150.
audit_findings.AUDIT-4: Subagent injection HOOK (enforce-tearags-search.sh) — the actual SUFFIX delivered to real subagents — had no post-search navigation hint AND no doc TOC branch. This is the production-impacting gap, not the search-cascade.md text.
audit_findings.AUDIT-5: navigation.prevSymbolId/nextSymbolId documented in isolated Chunk Navigation section, not connected to Decision Tree.
audit_findings.AUDIT-6 (Rerank conflict): Rerank Decision section line 199-207 prescribed 'hybrid_search → Read → semantic_search' for docs, contradicting doc TOC pattern. Read is heavier than necessary; find_symbol gives TOC + section content.

fixes_applied.Fix-1: Hoisted 'After-Search Navigation' block right after Principles in search-cascade.md — compact 7-row decision table covering truncated chunk / file structure / adjacent method / helper / doc TOC. Visible in first 50 lines (Wave 2 hoist pattern).
fixes_applied.Fix-2: Rewrote Rerank Decision 'Documentation search' flow: now TOC-first via find_symbol(relativePath:), then section drill via find_symbol(symbol: doc:<hash>); Read only for continuous prose.
fixes_applied.Fix-3: Subagent injection (search-cascade.md AND enforce-tearags-search.sh hook): added 'After ANY search returns a chunk...' block with 5 post-search navigation patterns + 'NEVER Read after find_symbol, NEVER re-run semantic_search on same area' anti-patterns. Hook is THE critical fix — it is what reaches production subagents.

evals.name: post-search-truncated-chunk
evals.prompt: Truncated chunk for Reranker#rerank received from semantic_search; need complete implementation.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'Reranker#rerank'). NOT Read, NOT another search.

old_suffix.reason: Existing 'Study a specific known symbol' branch sufficient

fixed_suffix.reason: Explicit 'Truncated method body → find_symbol with symbolId' rule

evals.name: post-search-need-file-structure
evals.prompt: Need overall structure of src/middleware/auth.ts after semantic_search returned one chunk.
evals.expected: find_symbol(relativePath: 'src/middleware/auth.ts'). NOT Read.

old_suffix.reason: Old rule had no explicit 'file structure' branch; agent inferred from 'Single-file scope'

evals.name: post-search-adjacent-via-navigation
evals.prompt: Navigate to navigation.prevSymbolId from hybrid_search result.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: navigation.prevSymbolId). NOT new search.

evals.name: post-search-deeper-into-helper
evals.prompt: Drill into helper validateAmount referenced inside a charge() chunk.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'validateAmount' or 'Validator#validateAmount').

evals.name: post-search-class-outline-for-discovery
evals.prompt: Need full public API of EnrichmentCoordinator after seeing constructor chunk.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'EnrichmentCoordinator') or find_symbol(relativePath: result.relativePath).

evals.name: doc-toc-by-path
evals.prompt: TOC of docs/architecture.md without reading full file.
evals.expected: find_symbol(relativePath: 'docs/architecture.md').

evals.name: doc-toc-from-search-result
evals.prompt: All sections of docs/signals.md given a chunk with parentSymbolId='doc:7c12d9b4e8a1'.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'doc:7c12d9b4e8a1').

baseline_no_rules.reason: Used relativePath instead of parentSymbolId — works but misses navigation shortcut

fixed_suffix.reason: Explicit 'Found a doc chunk → find_symbol(symbol: parentSymbolId)' rule

evals.name: doc-toc-multi-orientation
evals.prompt: TOCs of 3 docs in parallel for orientation.
evals.expected: 3 parallel find_symbol(relativePath:) calls.

evals.name: regression-direct-symbol-definition
evals.prompt: Show me the implementation of the Reranker class.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'Reranker').

evals.name: regression-behavioral-intent
evals.prompt: Find code that handles retry logic after embedding API failures.
evals.expected: semantic_search.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Picked hybrid_search instead of semantic_search for pure intent

evals.name: regression-todo-marker
evals.prompt: Find all TODO and FIXME comments in src/core/.
evals.expected: ripgrep with pattern 'TODO|FIXME'.

evals.name: regression-exhaustive-usage
evals.prompt: Find all callers of class PaymentProcessor.
evals.expected: hybrid_search query='PaymentProcessor'. NOT ripgrep, NOT find_symbol (returns def only).

evals.name: subagent-post-search-navigation
evals.prompt: [subagent: explore] Truncated chunk for rerank() — complete the analysis.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'Reranker#rerank') for full body.

evals.name: subagent-doc-toc
evals.prompt: [subagent: task-agent] v18→v19 section of docs/migration-guide.md via TOC first.
evals.expected: find_symbol(relativePath:) then find_symbol(symbol: doc:<hash>).

evals.name: implicit-explain-method
evals.prompt: User asks: How does Reranker.rerank work? Walk me through the algorithm.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'Reranker#rerank') → follow-up find_symbol on each helper referenced in body.

evals.name: implicit-explain-class
evals.prompt: User asks: Explain how PaymentProcessor handles failed transactions.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'PaymentProcessor') → drill into failure-handling methods.

evals.name: implicit-understand-doc
evals.prompt: User asks: What's in docs/architecture.md? Give me a summary.
evals.expected: find_symbol(relativePath:) for TOC → find_symbol(symbol: doc:hash) per section.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Added unnecessary Read on the file (heavy)

evals.name: implicit-trace-call
evals.prompt: User asks: Trace HTTP request → auth middleware → controller.
evals.expected: semantic_search → find_symbol chain along the request path.

evals.name: implicit-found-but-shallow
evals.prompt: EnrichmentCoordinator lifecycle (start/processing/shutdown) from a shallow first chunk.
evals.expected: find_symbol on outline + lifecycle methods + referenced collaborators (queue.drain, providers).

evals.name: implicit-find-similar-pattern
evals.prompt: User asks: Other places using retry-with-backoff pattern like Reranker#retryEmbedding.
evals.expected: find_symbol → find_similar.

evals.name: implicit-doc-section-deep-dive
evals.prompt: User asks: What does the migration guide say about v18→v19 sparse vector rebuild?
evals.expected: semantic_search OR find_symbol(relativePath:) → find_symbol(symbol: doc:hash) for section.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Used Read(offset/limit) for the section instead of find_symbol(symbol: doc:hash)

evals.name: implicit-multi-file-comparison
evals.prompt: User asks: Compare auth structure across 3 middleware files.
evals.expected: Parallel find_symbol(relativePath:) outlines + find_symbol(symbol:) per comparable method.

evals.name: breadth-different-subsystem
evals.prompt: After finding Reranker#retryEmbedding via semantic_search, user wants to see OTHER components with retry logic.
evals.expected: New semantic_search/hybrid_search with broader query, then find_symbol per interesting hit. NOT just find_symbol on first result.

baseline_no_rules.reason: Chose find_similar only — covers 'same pattern' but loses chain depth (no body-confirm per variant)

fixed_suffix_v2.reason: semantic_search('retry logic backoff') + per-hit find_symbol — perfect breadth→depth chain

evals.name: breadth-different-terminology
evals.prompt: Empty result for 'authorization middleware' — codebase uses different vocabulary.
evals.expected: Reformulate query with synonyms (guard, permission, auth) via new semantic_search. NOT fall back to ripgrep.

evals.name: breadth-polyglot-language-slice
evals.prompt: Polyglot TS+Python repo. Got TS results; user wants Python implementation of same feature.
evals.expected: New semantic_search with language='python' or pathPattern='**/*.py'. NOT find_symbol guesswork.

evals.name: breadth-pagination
evals.prompt: 10 hits returned; user wants ALL usages.
evals.expected: hybrid_search with offset=10, 20, 30 until empty. Pagination is breadth.

evals.name: breadth-different-angle-tests
evals.prompt: Found production code; user wants the tests for it.
evals.expected: New semantic_search with pathPattern='**/tests/**' or pathPattern='**/*.test.*'.

evals.name: depth-control-same-hit
evals.prompt: Got full PaymentProcessor#charge body; user re-asks same question more pointedly. TRAP: do NOT re-search.
evals.expected: Answer from existing body; no new search call. Optional find_symbol on referenced helper if user descends.

fixed_suffix_v2.grade: PASS — agent explicitly said 'Answer directly from already-visible body; no tool call'

evals.name: breadth-failed-query-reformulate
evals.prompt: Failed 'quota manager' query; chunk text reveals codebase uses 'rate budget tracker'.
evals.expected: New hybrid_search('rate budget tracker') with corrected vocabulary.

evals.name: depth-control-known-symbol-after-search
evals.prompt: Chunk reveals ConnectionPool class with initialize method; user wants initialize body.
evals.expected: find_symbol(symbol: 'ConnectionPool#initialize'). NOT a new search.

baseline_no_rules.reason: find_symbol('ConnectionPool.initialize') — wrong separator (. = static, # = instance); rules surface the convention

summary.old_suffix_pre_fix: ~22/22 with minor metaOnly misuse — but lacks explicit post-search navigation rule which matters in long real prompts
summary.fixed_suffix_post_fix: 22/22 — clean, with explicit doc TOC + post-search navigation branches
summary.fixed_suffix_v2_breadth_depth: 8/8 PASS on B1-B8 (breadth/depth cases). Confirms the softened rule still routes depth questions to find_symbol while explicitly allowing legitimate breadth re-searches (different subsystem, different terminology, polyglot language slice, pagination, different angle).
summary.iteration_7b_context: Initial Iteration 7 SUFFIX said 'NEVER re-run semantic_search on an area you already searched; navigate instead.' User pointed out this conflates depth (find_symbol territory) with breadth (legitimately needs new search). Iteration 7b replaced the absolute prohibition with explicit DEPTH vs BREADTH guidance + rule of thumb.
summary.key_insight: On clean eval prompts, Opus 4.7 selects tools correctly even without rules — the description of find_symbol is self-explanatory. The fix's value is NOT measurable pass-rate uplift on synthetic prompts; it is hardening for real-world long-prompt subagent contexts where the SUFFIX is competing for attention with task-specific instructions. Explicit 'After ANY search returns a chunk → navigate via find_symbol' converts a heuristic into a discoverable rule. Iteration 7b also demonstrates eval blind spots: 22/22 on Iter-7 cases would have masked breadth-shaped regressions — surfaced only by user design intuition.
